task_completion.description: Open the chinook.odb database in LibreOffice Base
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Base main window is visible with 'chinook.odb' displayed in the title bar or the database pane.

task_completion.description: Start creating a form for the Customer table
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Form Wizard or Form Design window is open, and the 'Customer' table is selected as the data source (e.g., in the 'Table or query' dropdown).

task_completion.description: Include all fields from the Customer table in the form
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Form Wizard, all available fields from the Customer table are moved to the 'Fields in the form' list, or in the Form Design view, all 13 fields (CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, etc.) are placed on the form canvas.

task_completion.description: Configure form properties to prevent additions, modifications, and deletions
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Form Properties' dialog is open on the 'Data' tab. The settings for 'Allow additions', 'Allow modifications', and 'Allow deletions' are all visibly set to 'No'.

task_completion.description: Save the form with the name 'Customer_ReadOnly'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main LibreOffice Base window shows the 'Forms' section selected on the left, and a form named exactly 'Customer_ReadOnly' is listed in the main pane.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Base GUI to create the form
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with LibreOffice Base menus, wizards, and dialogs rather than terminal commands modifying the .odb file directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the genuine LibreOffice Base application
integrity.visual_evidence: The visual interface matches LibreOffice Base, not a text editor, script editor, or web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not modify the underlying table schema or data
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening the table in edit mode or running SQL commands to alter the Customer table.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The database is located at /home/ga/chinook.odb. The target table is 'Customer', which contains 13 columns (CustomerId, FirstName, LastName, Company, Address, City, State, Country, PostalCode, Phone, Fax, Email, SupportRepId). The form must be named exactly 'Customer_ReadOnly'. To strictly prevent data modifications, the form properties 'Allow additions', 'Allow modifications', and 'Allow deletions' must all be set to 'No' (false) in the Data tab of the Form Properties.
